title: Trident PD record (pre-emptive decision)
description: One line of decisions.jsonl. A forward-looking design decision that pre-empts a predicted failure, informed by an external authority — NOT an observed failure. Kept separate from failures.jsonl so the CF SSOT only ever grows from real incidents (the Trident discipline). A PD is promoted to a CF the first time its failure is actually observed. Same sanitization rule as CF records: no personal paths, names, or private-company references.

id.description: PD-NNN. Separate number space from CF. Never reused.

date.description: ISO date the decision was made.

kind.description: Always 'decision' — this is not an observed failure.

scope.description: This ledger is meta-scoped: it records decisions about Trident ITSELF, never object-level decisions from a session Trident is watching. Enforced deterministically by validate_decisions.py via applied_in.

status.description: 'pre-emptive' = predicted, not yet seen. 'promoted' = the failure was observed and a CF now owns it. 'retired' = decision reversed/superseded.

guard.description: One-line rule now in place to pre-empt the failure.

pattern.description: The predicted failure mode this pre-empts. Generalized, no personal specifics.

grounds.description: WHERE/WHY/HOW used: the decision it justifies + how realized.

authority.description: External authorities that informed the decision (public references only). Prefer a STRUCTURED entry {source,url,accessed,grounds} so where/why/how is durable and never lost (a bare string is legacy). Distinguishes a PD from a hypothetical: grounded in current practice, not invented.

promotion_trigger.description: The concrete observed event that would convert this PD into a real CF.

applied_in.description: Repo-relative files changed to enact the decision. This is the meta-scope signal: every path must resolve inside Trident's own design tree (.claude/skills/, failures/, tests/, core root docs) and exist on disk, or validate_decisions.py rejects the record as object-level.

origin.description: Provenance note. Always makes explicit this is pre-emptive, not observed.
